name: hostapd-wpe

arguments.name: -s
arguments.description: Return EAP-Success messages after credentials are harvested

examples: hostapd-wpe logs all data to stdout and hostapd-wpe.log
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone https://github.com/OpenSecurityResearch/hostapd-wpe; apt-get install libssl-dev libnl-dev. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `hostapd-wpe logs all data to stdout and hostapd-wpe.log`. Further invocations from upstream: `hostapd-wpe hostapd-wpe.conf`. hostapd-wpe (Wireless Pwnage Edition) brad.antoniewicz@foundstone.com twitter: @bradanton ------------------------------------------.
documentation.when_to_use:
Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [wifi_2_4_ghz, wifi_5_ghz, shell_linux] in the Wireless category. Fits when the engagement needs: hostapd-wpe (Wireless Pwnage Edition)
brad.antoniewicz@foundstone.com
twitter: @brad_anton
------------------------------------------.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: hostapd-wpe (Wireless Pwnage Edition) brad.antoniewicz@foundstone.com twitter: @bradanton ------------------------------------------.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases:
When you need Wireless, use hostapd-wpe because hostapd-wpe (Wireless Pwnage Edition)
brad.antoniewicz@foundstone.com
twitter: @brad_anton
------------------------------------------.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `hostapd-wpe logs all data to stdout and hostapd-wpe.log` as documented upstream for hostapd-wpe.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `hostapd-wpe hostapd-wpe.conf` as documented upstream for hostapd-wpe.
When chaining Wireless tools, pair hostapd-wpe with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `hostapd-wpe --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
command_examples: hostapd-wpe logs all data to stdout and hostapd-wpe.log
